Title: my computer keeps removing Foxit as my default PDF
Notes:
Received a request from Lisa De La Fuente reporting that her computer keeps removing Foxit as the default PDF application and prompting Adobe to become the default viewer.

Connected to Lisa’s workstation remotely to investigate the issue. Verified the PDF default application settings and confirmed that Adobe Acrobat was prompting to become the default PDF viewer. Adjusted the settings within Adobe to disable the prompt asking to become the default PDF application.

Using administrator credentials, configured the system to set Foxit PDF Editor as the default application for .pdf files through Windows default application settings to ensure PDFs open with Foxit going forward.

Verified the changes by opening multiple PDF files to confirm that Foxit remained the default viewer and Adobe no longer prompted for default access.

After completing the configuration, sent a confirmation email to Lisa informing her that the issue has been resolved and advising her to contact support if the issue occurs again.

Issue has been successfully resolved and Foxit is now set as the default PDF application on her computer.

Title: Default PDF- Again it is saying Foxit is not my default PDF reader
Notes: As per request by user connected to machine verified Investigated recurring Foxit PDF Reader default preview tool popup on the workstation. Verified that Foxit was already set as the default application for .pdf and related file types in Windows. Determined that the prompt was being triggered by Foxit’s internal default viewer check rather than Windows file associations. Implemented a registry modification to disable the Foxit default viewer check (HKCU\Software\Foxit Software\Foxit PDF Reader\Preferences\General → CheckDefaultPDFReader = 0). Restarted Foxit to apply the change and confirmed the popup is no longer appearing. System is functioning normally; will continue to monitor to ensure the issue does not recur.

Title: Huntress Low Incident | LOW - Incident on WKORX61 (ORX - Ortho Xpress)
Notes:
We received a security alert indicating that a password-protected file (Passwords and Login Info.xlsx) exists on system WKORX61 at the following path:
C:\Users\DAyala\OneDrive - Orthopress\Accounting\Accounting Logins\Passwords and Login Info.xlsx.

This file contains passwords and is stored in an unencrypted state, which poses a significant security risk. Such files are highly vulnerable if attackers gain access to the system.

Actions Taken:

  1. Deborah Ayala confirmed that the file has a password assigned, and it is used each time the file is opened.

  2. We advised encrypting the file using a strong password via the File > Protect Using Password option in Excel (Tools → Protect → Encrypt → Encrypt with Password).

  3. If the file is no longer needed, we recommended deleting it to prevent potential security risks.

  4. User Response: Deborah confirmed that the file does have a password and she uses it each time the file is opened.

Next Steps:

  • Confirm if the file will be encrypted or deleted to ensure security.

  • Monitor for any further follow-up from the user regarding encryption or deletion.

  • User replied hence closed the ticket.

Title: DESKTOP Alert - Critical - WKORX43 Disk Utilization
Notes: As per the alert connected to WKORX43, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files.. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

Title: Re: Technijian | myArchive
Notes:
Issue Description:
Mike reported being unable to access the archive server and was unable to access his email.

Investigation & Actions Taken:

  1. Initial Investigation:
    • Upon receiving the report, the team began investigating the issue.
    • Identified the problem as related to SSL certificate configuration on the client server.
  2. Troubleshooting:
    • Verified the installation of the SSL certificate and discovered it was missing or improperly linked.
    • Reinstalled the SSL certificate (star_technijian_com.crt) and intermediate certificates (DigiCertCA.crt and TrustedRoot.crt), ensuring they were properly imported into the Local Machine Certificate Store.
    • Checked the private key linkage, confirming the correct private key was attached to the SSL certificate.
    • Restarted IIS to apply changes.
  3. Confirmation:
    • After installing the certificates and restarting IIS, the connection to the archive server was tested and confirmed to be successful.
  4. Verification with Mike:
    • Contacted Mike for confirmation, and he was able to successfully log into the archive server and access his email without any issues.

Resolution:
The issue was resolved after correctly installing and linking the SSL certificate on the client server. Mike has successfully accessed the archive server and his email.

Ticket Status: Closed

Title: WKORX45,WKORX35,WKORX37,WKORX72 & WKORX49 has Missing patch while last run
Notes:
Devices WKORX45, WKORX35, WKORX37, WKORX72, and WKORX49 were identified as non-compliant due to missing patches.

Logged in to the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on all affected systems. Proceeded with patch remediation by installing the following updates:

  • Microsoft 365 Apps for Business (Current Channel, x64) – Version 2603 (Build 19822.20114)
  • 2026-03 Cumulative Update Preview for Windows 11, Version 25H2 for x64-based Systems – KB5079391 (OS Build 26200.8116)
  • Adobe Acrobat Reader DC (x64) – Version 26.001.21346

After installation, performed a manual patch scan on all systems to verify successful deployment and to clear the non-compliant status.

Post validation, confirmed that all patches have been successfully applied and no high vulnerabilities remain. All systems are now compliant as per patch management policy.
